g.text: guards against: the prover discharges (Qed.) an adjacent weaker claim (e.g.
right-identity n+0=n instead of n+m=m+n) under the intended theorem name, or
catches: a vacuous or inconsistent statement
(e.g. a false hypothesis that trivialises the
goal) — via a vacuous-hypothesis mutation that
should be rejected; the kernel/axiom audit
catches: discharges an adjacent weaker theorem
under the intended name — via identity-flip
mutations the kernel must reject; type
cross-check of the proof against the statement
catches: omits a mutation for some scope
clause — via Gate C requires every manifest
clause to appear in some mutation's
